network.title: Network Settings
network.lanWarningText: LAN mode exposes your API keys to the local network. Only use this in trusted environments.

auth.title: Authenticate before entering
auth.lead: The 8080 admin UI now requires a username and password, and the Codex relay on 18100 only accepts managed keys created here.
auth.noteBody: Before login, only initialization and login endpoints stay open. RPC traffic and event streams are blocked.

placeholders.setupToken: Required for first-time public setup. Leave blank for local setup.
placeholders.newUsername: Leave blank to keep current username
placeholders.newPassword: Leave blank to keep current password

errors.passwordMismatch: The two passwords do not match

setup.title: Create the admin account
setup.description: Before using the admin UI for the first time, set the 8080 admin username and password. First-time public setup also requires the setup token. You will be logged in immediately after setup.
setup.tokenHint: For first-time public setup, enter the setup token printed in the server logs. Leave it blank if you are initializing directly on the server itself.

login.title: Sign in to the admin UI
login.description: Enter the admin username and password to unlock the management UI and protected APIs.

security.description: Manage the 8080 admin account credentials. Relay access keys are managed from the Keys page in the sidebar.
security.adminCardDescription: Current signed-in account: {username}. Leave the new username or password blank to keep the current value.
security.keepHint: Leave blank to keep the current value
security.keysCardDescription: The Codex relay on 18100 validates these keys and no longer accepts arbitrary secrets.
security.createPlaceholder: For example: Local dev / CI / automation
security.loadKeysFailed: Failed to load Codex key list
security.empty: No Codex keys yet. Generate one to start using the relay.
security.oneTimeTitle: The full key is shown only once
security.oneTimeDescription: Copy and store it now. The list only shows a masked value afterwards.

modelWhitelist.label: Supported Models
modelWhitelist.tooltip: Declare which models this provider supports. Supports exact match (claude-sonnet-4) and wildcard (claude-*)

modelMapping.label: Model Mapping
modelMapping.tooltip: Map CLI requested model names to provider-specific names. Supports wildcard expansion

examples.title: Examples:
examples.wildcard: Wildcard mapping, auto expands claude-sonnet-4 → anthropic/claude-sonnet-4

hero.title: Stay on top of AI coding supply.
hero.lead: Review provider status, relay switches, and today's usage in one place.

relayToggle.label: Claude proxy
relayToggle.tooltip: Route traffic through the local relay to manage API keys centrally.

customCli.selectToolFirst: Please select a CLI tool first
customCli.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete {name}? This action cannot be undone.
customCli.baseUrlFieldPlaceholder: Base URL field path, e.g., env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL
customCli.proxyHint: Proxy injection writes the local proxy address to the specified config file fields
customCli.configRequired: Please add at least one valid config file path
customCli.invalidProxyTarget: Invalid proxy injection target config file

importConfig.empty: Everything from cc-switch is already in sync

providers.codexSwitchDisabled: Enable hosted Codex to use this switch

placeholders.name: e.g. AICoding.sh
placeholders.apiEndpoint: Leave blank for the platform default, for example /v1/responses on Claude-compatible OpenAI relays
placeholders.availabilityTestModel: Leave empty to use platform default

hints.level: Lower numbers = higher priority. Level 1 providers are tried first, then Level 2, etc.
hints.apiEndpoint: Override the platform default endpoint. Leave blank for default (Claude: /v1/messages for Anthropic, /v1/responses for OpenAI Compatible; Codex: /responses). For GLM models use /v1/chat/completions
hints.upstreamProtocol: Specify the upstream API protocol. Auto detects from the endpoint; OpenAI Compatible converts Claude Messages requests to OpenAI Responses or Chat Completions
hints.availabilityMonitor: Enable background health checks for this provider. Checks are performed periodically to monitor availability.
hints.connectivityAutoBlacklist: Automatically blacklist this provider when availability checks fail repeatedly
hints.availabilityAdvancedConfig: Advanced configuration (test model, endpoint, timeout) can be edited on the Availability page
hints.availabilityTestModel: Model to use for health checks. Leave empty to use platform default.
hints.availabilityTestEndpoint: API endpoint for health checks. Leave empty to use platform default.
hints.availabilityTimeout: Timeout for health check requests in milliseconds (default: 15000)
hints.connectivityCheck: Legacy field, migrated to Availability Monitoring
hints.connectivityTestModel: Select a model for availability checks, or leave empty to use platform default
hints.connectivityTestEndpoint: Select or enter API endpoint path
hints.connectivityAuthType: Default is Bearer. Use X-API-Key for Anthropic-style APIs. Enter custom header name in the text field if needed.

upstreamProtocol.autoDesc: Automatically detect protocol based on API endpoint
upstreamProtocol.openaiChatDesc: Use OpenAI Responses / Chat Completions with Claude Messages format conversion

form.confirmDeleteMessage: Are you sure you want to remove {name}? This action cannot be undone.

errors.invalidUrl: Please enter a valid API URL

blacklist.unblockAndResetHint: Unblock while keeping current level; degradation timer restarts
blacklist.resetLevelHint: Reset level only, keep blacklist status

errors.loadProvidersFailed: Failed to load providers for {tab} tab

logs.title: Request logs
logs.subtitle: Inspect recent relay activity for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini providers.

label.switchNotifyHint: Send system notification when provider switches or gets blacklisted
label.roundRobinHint: When enabled, providers at the same Level take turns handling requests; when disabled, always starts from the first
label.codexStreamGuardHint: Intercept Codex 200 streams with no initial useful content and retry before the client sees them
label.autoConnectivityTestHint: Automatically check availability of monitored providers every minute
label.enableBlacklistHint: When disabled, providers will not be automatically blacklisted
label.enableLevelBlacklistHint: When enabled, use graduated blacklist mechanism (L1-L5); when disabled, use fixed duration

subLabel.sb_assistant_access: Accessibility permission is required to operate clipboard contents
subLabel.sb_disk_access: Full disk access is required to implement file preview

import.nothingToImport: Nothing to import (all may have been imported)
import.fileNotFound: Config file not found, please check the path

hero.title: Manage Gemini Providers
hero.lead: Configure API providers for Gemini CLI, supporting Google Official and third-party relay services.

list.empty: No providers configured. Click a preset card to add one.

form.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete {name}? This action cannot be undone.
form.name: Provider name

shortcut.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcut.helper: Record shortcuts to control the app from anywhere.

descriptions.openMain: Must include at least one modifier and a main key
descriptions.openPreferences: Use a distinct combo to avoid conflicts

deeplink.title: Import Provider from Deep Link
deeplink.preview: Please confirm the following information before importing:

cliConfig.title: CLI Configuration
cliConfig.noCustomFields: No custom fields. Click + to add one.
cliConfig.keyConflictLocked: This key conflicts with a locked field
cliConfig.keyConflictPreset: This key conflicts with a preset field
cliConfig.setAsTemplate: Set as default template for new providers
cliConfig.restoreConfirm: Are you sure you want to restore the default configuration? This will overwrite current settings.
cliConfig.jsonParseError: Invalid JSON format, please check your input
cliConfig.smartPasteFailed: Unrecognized paste, please use JSON / TOML / .env content
cliConfig.previewParseError: Invalid config format, please check syntax
cliConfig.saveAllFailed: All config files failed to save

keys.title: Key Management
keys.subtitle: Manage Code Switch relay keys and inspect call and token usage trends for each key.
keys.managementHint: Create, copy, rename, or delete Codex / Claude relay keys.
keys.usageAllKeys: Shows usage for every key in the selected time range, with a separate color per key.
